synopsis: The book follows Apple after Steve Jobs’s death through the contrasting leadership of design chief Jony Ive and operations executive Tim Cook. It traces the company’s expansion into a trillion-dollar enterprise while examining tensions between design and finance, the development of products such as the Apple Watch, Ive’s eventual departure, and the organizational choices that reshape Apple’s culture.

The book traces the intellectual history of risk from ancient reliance on fate and divination to mathematical methods for measuring uncertainty and making decisions. Moving through probability, statistics, insurance, utility, regression, portfolio theory, and behavioral research, it follows the mathematicians, merchants, gamblers, and economists whose ideas transformed uncertainty into a central problem of modern finance and society.

The autobiography recounts Dyson’s development from art and engineering student into an inventor and manufacturer, centering on the prolonged creation and commercialization of the bagless Dual Cyclone vacuum cleaner. It follows repeated prototypes, licensing disputes, financial setbacks, and resistance from established appliance companies, alongside earlier inventions and the decisions involved in building an independent design-led business.

The book presents a practical framework for building applications with existing foundation models, distinguishing AI engineering from traditional machine-learning development and mapping the surrounding model, data, evaluation, and deployment stack. It covers evaluation, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation, agents, fine-tuning, dataset construction, inference optimization, guardrails, routing, caching, monitoring, and user feedback, with attention to cost, latency, reliability, and model selection.

The biography follows Hamilton from his orphaned Caribbean childhood through the Revolutionary War, the Constitutional debates, and his service as the first United States Treasury secretary. It connects his work on the Federalist Papers, national finance, a central bank, and federal power with his marriage, political feuds, public scandal, and fatal duel with Aaron Burr, presenting the early republic through his career.

The biography traces Alexander from his upbringing at the Macedonian court and accession after Philip II’s death through the conquest of Persia and campaigns reaching India. It examines his battlefield command, political calculations, treatment of conquered peoples, pursuit of personal glory, increasingly autocratic rule, and death in Babylon, placing the military record alongside the violence and instability of his empire-building.

sources.title: Alexander of Macedon, 356–323 B.C. | University of California Press

synopsis: The study reconstructs how the Macedonian army moved and survived across Alexander’s campaigns by calculating food, water, animal fodder, transport capacity, marching rates, terrain, climate, and the burden of noncombatants. It uses those constraints to explain strategic choices from Greece and Anatolia through Egypt, Mesopotamia, Iran, Central Asia, and India, showing logistics as a limiting force on conquest.

sources.title: Alexander the Great and the Logistics of the Macedonian Army | University of California Press

synopsis: The book argues that current artificial intelligence solves well-structured path problems but lacks the capacity to formulate new representations for unfamiliar, ill-structured situations. Drawing on cognitive science, it assesses machine learning, neural networks, and the Turing Test while identifying perception, analogy, ambiguity, common sense, creativity, and insight as components required for artificial general intelligence.

sources.title: Algorithms Are Not Enough | The MIT Press

synopsis: In Wonderland, Alice follows a White Rabbit underground and encounters abrupt changes in size, a disappearing cat, a perpetual tea party, and the Queen of Hearts’s chaotic court. Through the Looking-Glass sends her across a chessboard world of reversed logic, living game pieces, wordplay, and shifting identity until she becomes a queen and wakes from the dream.

sources.title: Alice’s Adventures in Wonderland and Through the Looking-Glass | Penguin Random House
